title: Forms
intro: Three layouts cover every form in the app: vertical by default, two-column for wide surfaces, inline for filter bars. One label recipe and three spacing tiers do the rest.

blocks.title: Form laws
blocks.intro: Every form in the app follows these. Layout, spacing, and submit wiring are not per-screen decisions.

items.rule: Labels sit above inputs in every layout — vertical, two-column, and inline alike.
items.why: Horizontal label layouts break at narrow widths and force ragged label-to-input alignment as label lengths vary.

items.rule: Use exactly three spacing tiers: 4px label-to-input (space-1), 16px field-to-field (space-4), 24px section-to-section and card padding (space-5).
items.why: A strict hierarchy is what lets the eye separate sections from fields. Flatten it and the form reads as one undifferentiated stack.

items.rule: Labels are title case Texto p2 at 14px, weight 500. Required fields append a red asterisk colored var(--status-danger-solid).
items.why: One label voice across the app. Mixed casing, weights, or categories inside a single form makes it look assembled from parts.

items.rule: Validation errors render directly below the offending input as Texto p2 with appearance="error".
items.why: Proximity binds the message to the field. Errors collected at the top of a form force the user to map messages back to inputs.

items.rule: Submit via GraviButton onClick={() => form.submit()} — never htmlType="submit".
items.why: htmlType only submits when the button renders inside the form element — drawer and modal footers do not, so onFinish never fires and the bug surfaces as a dead Save button. form.submit() runs validation then onFinish from anywhere.

items.rule: Reactive dependencies between fields use Form.useWatch, never form.getFieldValue in render.
items.why: getFieldValue does not trigger re-renders, so dependent fields and conditional sections go stale until something else repaints.

items.rule: Money inputs and copy are decimal dollars — $0.0100/gal — never cents symbols.
items.why: Gravitate's pricing domain operates in decimal dollars end to end. A cents symbol anywhere corrupts copy, axis labels, and seed data.

items.rule: Pick layout by purpose: vertical for create/edit surfaces, two-column when the surface is wide and fields are short, inline only for filter bars and toolbars.
items.why: Layout signals intent. A two-column drawer form or a vertical filter bar both fight their container.

blocks.title: Vertical form (default)
blocks.caption: The default layout: labels above inputs, 4px label gap, 16px between fields. Required fields carry a red asterisk; the error sits directly under the email input.

blocks.title: Multi-section vertical form
blocks.caption: Sections get an h5 heading and a hairline divider, separated by 24px (space-5). Fields inside each section keep the 16px rhythm.

blocks.title: Two-column horizontal form
blocks.caption: Two flex:1 columns with a 16px gap. Labels stay above inputs; every other spacing rule is unchanged from the vertical layout.

blocks.title: Inline filter bar
blocks.caption: Filter-bar form: fixed-width fields, labels above, items bottom-aligned so the action button lines up with the input row.

blocks.title: Field anatomy and spacing tiers
blocks.caption: The single source of truth for form spacing: 4px (space-1) label-to-input, 16px (space-4) field-to-field, 24px (space-5) section-to-section, annotated on a live form.

blocks.title: Do / Don't: one label recipe
blocks.caption: Do: every label uses the same category, weight, size, and casing. Don't: mixed label styles in one form — it reads as disjointed even when the fields are identical.

blocks.title: Do / Don't: spacing hierarchy
blocks.caption: Do: 24px padding and section gaps over 16px field gaps over 4px label gaps. Don't: flat 10px everywhere — sections blur into fields.

blocks.title: Form spacing tokens
blocks.intro: Forms use four tokens. Nothing in a form is spaced with an arbitrary value.

rows.use: Gap between controls in an inline filter bar; gap between footer buttons
Field to field gap; gap between columns in a two-column layout
Section to section gap; form padding inside a card or drawer

blocks.title: Structure
blocks.body:
Forms live inside a card, drawer, or panel with `var(--space-5)` padding. Group related fields into sections: an h5 heading (`<Texto category="h5" weight="600">`), fields at 16px rhythm, and a hairline divider (`borderTop: 1px solid`) between sections at 24px.

Use antd `Form` with `layout="vertical"` so labels render above inputs, and `Form.Item` for every field — it owns the label, the required asterisk, and the inline error for free. Footer actions sit in a right-aligned `Horizontal gap={8}`: secondary action first, primary (`theme1`) last.

blocks.title: Canonical form skeleton
blocks.note: GraviButton takes buttonText — never children — and submits via onClick={() => form.submit()}, never htmlType="submit". Money fields are decimal dollars: $0.0100/gal.

rows.name: Vertical
rows.when: Default. Drawers, modals, and any create/edit surface.
rows.code: <Form layout="vertical"> with one <Vertical gap={16}> per section

rows.name: Two-column
rows.when: Wide panels with many short fields. Never in a drawer.
rows.code: <Horizontal gap={16}> wrapping two <Vertical flex="1" gap={16}> columns

rows.name: Inline filter bar
rows.when: Table toolbars, search rows, compact control strips.
rows.code: Flex row, alignItems: flex-end, gap var(--space-2), fixed-width fields

items.do: Use one label recipe everywhere: title case, Texto p2, 14px, weight 500, red asterisk on required fields.
items.dont: Mix casing, categories, weights, or label-to-input gaps across fields in one form.
items.why: Labels are the loudest repeated element in a form — any inconsistency multiplies.

items.do: Keep the spacing hierarchy: 24px sections > 16px fields > 4px labels.
items.dont: Flatten everything to one gap value.
items.why: Without distinct tiers there is no visual grouping; sections and fields read as one list.

items.do: Write money as decimal dollars: $0.0100/gal, InputNumber with precision={4} and a $ prefix.
items.dont: Use cents symbols or cents-denominated inputs anywhere.
items.why: Gravitate prices in decimal dollars; a single cents symbol in copy or seed data breaks consistency with every other surface.

items.do: Submit with <GraviButton theme1 buttonText="Save" onClick={() => form.submit()} />.
items.why: Both are on the repo's Common Mistakes list: htmlType only submits when the button sits inside the form element (drawer and modal footers do not), and type="primary" bypasses the GraviButton theme system — use theme1.

items.title: GraviButton takes buttonText, not children
items.detail: Self-close and pass buttonText="Save". Excalibrr 4.x renders only buttonText — <GraviButton>Save</GraviButton> comes out as an empty button; 5.x falls back to children, but buttonText is the API everywhere. Primary styling is the boolean theme1 prop, success is the boolean success prop — there is no theme string prop, and antd's type="primary" is on the repo's Common Mistakes list.

items.title: Never submit via htmlType
items.detail: htmlType="submit" passes through to the underlying antd Button but only submits when that button renders inside the form element — drawer and modal footers do not, so the Save button goes dead. Wire onClick={() => form.submit()}; it runs validation and then onFinish from anywhere.

items.title: form.getFieldValue in render is stale
items.detail: It reads the store without subscribing, so conditional fields and derived labels do not update when the value changes. Use const x = Form.useWatch('x', form) for anything render-reactive.

items.title: Texto has no p3 or h6, and 'secondary' is blue
items.detail: Valid categories: p1, p2, label, heading, heading-small, h1–h5. Error copy is appearance="error"; gray helper text is appearance="medium" — appearance="secondary" renders blue, not gray.
